questions.question: Explain the three main types of computer system architectures: single-processor, multiprocessor, and clustered systems. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Single-processor: One main general-purpose CPU, may have special-purpose processors
Multiprocessor: Two or more CPUs sharing bus, memory, and peripherals (tightly coupled)
Clustered: Multiple complete systems coupled via LAN sharing storage for high availability
Key differences in coupling, resource sharing, and failure handling

questions.question: Compare Symmetric Multiprocessing (SMP) and Asymmetric Multiprocessing (AMP). (3 marks)
keyPoints: SMP: All processors are peers, each runs OS, no master-slave relationship
AMP: Master processor controls system, assigns tasks to slave processors
SMP is more common in modern systems; AMP is simpler to design

questions.question: What are the advantages of multiprocessor systems? Explain graceful degradation and fault tolerance. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Increased throughput: N processors can do more work than one
Economy of scale: Shared peripherals and storage cost less than separate systems
Increased reliability: Failure of one processor does not halt the system
Graceful degradation: Continued service proportional to surviving hardware
Fault tolerance: System continues operation even after any single component failure

questions.question: Differentiate between symmetric and asymmetric clustering. (3 marks)
keyPoints: Asymmetric: One active server, one hot-standby monitoring node; standby takes over on failure
Symmetric: All nodes run applications and monitor each other; more efficient resource use
Symmetric clustering is preferred as it utilizes all available hardware
